title: Washington State House and Senate Put Forth Bills to Hike Tax Rates on Fuel Licensees
content: Under Washington State's Biennial Transportation Budget Proposals, the Senate has proposed a 6-cent increase in the tax rate on fuel licensees, while the House has proposed a 9-cent increase per gallon of fuel. These hikes are proposed to take effect on July 1, 2025.  Both Chambers have proposed that the tax rate be adjusted annually for inflation from July 1, 2026. Beginning July 1, 2025, the House bill has also proposed an additional and cumulative tax rate of 3 cents per gallon of special fuel on fuel licensees.
author: Analysts Account
